stories.title: Repository access and reader attention became explicit plans.
stories.brief: Baxtori now separates authorized inventory from inspectable caches, records inaccessible choices, and packs qualifying findings into a visible reading budget.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: scope and omission are now first-class evidence instead of compiler folklore
stories.whatChanged: The collector builds an authorized source plan from configured caches, repository inventory, reader selection, and per-repository modes. A separate selection policy ranks qualifying findings, fits them to a reading budget, and publishes included, deferred, excluded, quiet, and inaccessible decisions with the edition.
stories.whyItMatters: A selected repository can no longer silently become a code claim when its source is unavailable, and readers can see why meaningful work was included or omitted instead of reverse-engineering a fixed story quota.
stories.verify: Select one cached repository and one uncached repository, export inventory, collect, then confirm the cached repository is inspected, the uncached repository is listed as inaccessible, and every published story is accounted for in the selection ledger.
stories.tradeoff: The exporter currently fetches feedback and repository inventory in one Promise.all; if the inventory function is not deployed, the export aborts before the collector can use its intentional legacy-cache fallback.
stories.evidence: 5 origin-backed feature commits plus reviewed head · canonical repository identity, authorized source planning, explicit review modes, budgeted selection, and reader-visible decisions

codeEvidence.title: Classify every requested repository before collection
codeEvidence.why: The plan distinguishes configured caches, metadata-only repositories, authorization gaps, and muted repositories while allowing the pre-inventory legacy path only when no current inventory exists.

codeEvidence.title: Pack qualified findings into a visible budget
codeEvidence.why: Candidates are ordered by explicit priority, included while budget remains, and otherwise retained as deferred or excluded decisions instead of disappearing.

codeEvidence.title: Bind compiler input to feedback and inventory together
codeEvidence.why: The exporter queries both account-scoped feedback and authorized repository inventory, canonicalizes repository identities, and writes one ignored compiler input.

stories.title: The reading loop now remembers topics, questions, and history.
stories.brief: Watched evidence and exact code questions survive editions, while one Continue queue mixes unread stories, map areas, open questions, and unfinished reviews.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: comprehension state became durable without loosening its evidence address
stories.whatChanged: Topic threads and follow-up questions now retain repository, base and head commits, path, and bounded lines. Active watches and queued questions feed the compiler, archived editions form a filterable history, and the Continue planner ranks unread stories, re-reviews, map areas, and open questions into the reader's chosen attention window.
stories.whyItMatters: A useful question no longer vanishes with the weekly page, and returning readers can resume a concrete evidence trail rather than reconstructing where they stopped.
stories.verify: Watch one code excerpt, queue a question on its exact lines, mark another map area for revisit, then reload and confirm Continue contains all three items within the selected time window and History can find the original topic.
stories.tradeoff: Continue priorities and minute estimates are hard-coded heuristics, so the queue is deterministic but not yet calibrated from observed reading behavior; low-ranked items may remain unseen in short windows.
stories.evidence: 7 origin-backed feature commits plus reviewed head · exact evidence addresses, durable topic threads, queued questions, unified Continue planning, edition history, and reader-chosen attention windows

codeEvidence.title: Keep topic questions attached to exact evidence
codeEvidence.why: Evidence parsing requires canonical repository identity, full commit hashes, a safe path, and a bounded line range before a thread or question can enter the durable loop.

codeEvidence.title: Merge unfinished comprehension work into one queue
codeEvidence.why: Unread stories, unguided re-reviews, mapped areas, and open questions receive explicit priorities and minutes before the queue is sorted and packed to budget.

codeEvidence.title: Build a canonical, filterable edition history
codeEvidence.why: History deduplicates edition IDs, canonicalizes renamed repositories, sorts newest-first, and filters by repository, topic, or text.

stories.title: Capture became resumable without widening vault authority.
stories.brief: The Clipper checkpoints multi-item saves under one session and pairs through a short-lived grant for a revocable device credential.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: recoverable intake and least-authority capture reinforce each other
stories.whatChanged: The extension gained tab, URL-list, bookmark, and multi-image X intake with persisted checkpoints and a shared captureSessionId. Owner routes now require the deployment key, while a ten-minute one-use grant gives the Clipper a hashed, revocable device credential limited to capture.
stories.whyItMatters: Interrupted batches retain progress and attribution, while the browser extension can save into a private vault without inheriting the owner's master key.
stories.verify: Pair one Clipper, start a three-item batch with one bad URL, reload and resume, confirm two references share the session, then revoke the device and verify its next capture is rejected.
stories.tradeoff: The vault remains a single-owner bearer-secret model, and session summaries are inferred from recent successful references, so failed or skipped items and original expected counts are not reconstructed.
stories.evidence: 6 origin-backed feature commits plus reviewed head · one-use pairing, revocable device credentials, checkpointed bulk intake, rich X parsing, and inferred session review

codeEvidence.title: Separate owner and device secrets
codeEvidence.why: Owner comparison, random device tokens, one-use pairing codes, secret hashing, and allowed origins are centralized in one security module.

codeEvidence.title: Checkpoint every batch item under one session
codeEvidence.why: Each payload receives the batch job ID, success and failure totals are tracked separately, and the next index is persisted after every attempt so interruption does not restart completed work.

codeEvidence.title: Reconstruct the owner's recent capture groups
codeEvidence.why: The session mutation groups non-deleted references by captureSessionId, preserves an existing review state, and upserts a stable session record for later inspection.

stories.title: Retrieval gained reusable context and reviewable enrichment.
stories.brief: Projects, boards, tags, and saved searches preserve why a reference matters, while suggestions and related results expose their reasons before the owner accepts them.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: both organization and enrichment keep meaning inspectable at the relationship boundary
stories.whatChanged: Ourchival added boards, tags, projectReferences with per-use notes, bounded batch actions, and saved searches with field-level match excerpts. A separate enrichment ledger produces pending tag suggestions and reason-bearing related-reference scores rather than mutating taxonomy silently.
stories.whyItMatters: One asset can support several projects without duplication, and both search hits and heuristic recommendations retain the stored context that justified their appearance.
stories.verify: Attach two references to a project with a reason, save a search for it, accept one suggested tag, then confirm search and Related references show labeled match or ranking reasons.
stories.tradeoff: Retrieval remains bounded application-layer scanning and enrichment uses hand-tuned English rules; older rare matches can require paging, and the scores should not be read as learned semantic relevance.
stories.evidence: 10 origin-backed feature commits plus reviewed head · reusable project context, bounded rich search, observable enrichment jobs, pending suggestions, and reason-bearing relatedness

codeEvidence.title: Reuse references with context on the relationship
codeEvidence.why: The bounded batch mutation delegates each reference to an upsert that preserves a single project-reference pair and stores project-specific reason and notes.

codeEvidence.title: Return the field that justified a search hit
codeEvidence.why: Search checks reference, source-snapshot, tag, board, project, and reuse fields, then returns compact labeled excerpts instead of a bare Boolean match.

codeEvidence.title: Bound the cost of rich relationship search
codeEvidence.why: The catalog pages newest-first, hydrates search context only for filtered candidates, and stops after a fixed multiple of the requested page size while returning the continuation cursor.

codeEvidence.title: Derive bounded suggestions from weighted owned context
codeEvidence.why: Title and notes receive the strongest weight, source context contributes less, existing tags are excluded, and deterministic concept rules precede token-frequency candidates.

stories.title: Browsing no longer depends on serving every original.
stories.brief: A claimed background job turns stored Drive or Convex originals into bounded WebP previews, thumbnails, hashes, dimensions, and palette data.
stories.verdict: Worth studying: portable originals and fast browsing are separated without discarding provenance
stories.whatChanged: A scheduled Node action now claims media_derivatives jobs, loads a private Drive or Convex original, enforces byte and pixel limits, generates two oriented WebP sizes, computes content and perceptual hashes plus dominant colors, stores the outputs, and atomically marks the asset and job ready.
stories.whyItMatters: Reliquary can prefer small private derivatives for grids and inspection while the archival original remains in its chosen provider and supplies visual-similarity data without repeated browser analysis.
stories.verify: Enqueue one stored image without derivatives, wait for the worker, then confirm the asset has previewStorageId, thumbStorageId, dimensions, SHA-256 contentHash, 64-bit perceptualHash, colors, and derivativeStatus ready while the original provider fields remain unchanged.
stories.tradeoff: Only stored originals are eligible, failed attempts are not automatically retried without a forced enqueue, and a failure after writing one or both blobs but before the completion mutation can leave unreferenced storage objects for later cleanup.
stories.evidence: 1 origin-backed feature commit plus reviewed head · idempotent queueing, claimed Sharp worker, bounded inputs, private outputs, and observable failure state

codeEvidence.title: Queue one active derivative job per stored asset
codeEvidence.why: The mutation reuses queued or running work, records previous outcomes unless force is requested, marks the asset processing, and schedules the internal worker only for a new job.

codeEvidence.title: Claim, transform, hash, and commit the result
codeEvidence.why: The worker claims queued work before decoding, creates bounded derivatives and visual fingerprints in parallel, stores both files, and completes or fails the same job explicitly.

codeEvidence.title: Reject unsafe or unsuitable originals before decoding
codeEvidence.why: The loader accepts only private Drive or Convex storage responses, requires an image content type, and enforces the 25 MB limit both from headers and actual bytes.

included.reason: Material product-loop change with exact origin-backed implementation evidence.
Material product-loop change with exact origin-backed implementation evidence.
Material behavior change with exact origin-backed implementation evidence.
Material behavior change with exact origin-backed implementation evidence.
Material behavior change with exact origin-backed implementation evidence.

excluded.title: Scroll-first reader proposal
excluded.reason: Documentation-only proposal with no committed behavioral implementation.

excluded.title: Placeholder cleanup commits
excluded.reason: No-op placeholder additions and removals did not materially change behavior.

quiet.reason: No origin-backed commits were collected in the review window.

inaccessible.reason: Selected by the reader but absent from the configured fetch caches and current exported inventory.
Selected by the reader but absent from the configured fetch caches and current exported inventory.
Selected by the reader but absent from the configured fetch caches and current exported inventory.
